 {"id":"caam-04mb","title":"[FEATURE] JSON Output Consistency: Audit CLI commands and add missing --json flags","description":"BACKGROUND: Some commands have --json output (status, cooldown list), others don't. Inconsistent for scripting/automation.

AUDIT NEEDED:
- Which commands have --json?
- Which should have it but don't?
- What's the JSON schema pattern? (envelope vs raw data)

KNOWN COMMANDS:
- backup - no --json
- activate/switch/use - no --json
- status - has --json? (check)
- ls/list - no --json
- delete - no --json
- clear - no output, probably doesn't need --json
- cooldown set/list/clear - cooldown list has --json
- paths - no --json
- usage - has --json? (check)
- history (proposed) - will have --json

KEY FILES:
- cmd/caam/cmd/*.go (all command files)

APPROACH:
1. Audit all commands for --json support
2. Document current JSON schema patterns
3. Add --json to commands that produce structured output
4. Document schemas in help text or README

CONSIDERATIONS:
- Commands that just print messages (clear, uninstall) may not need --json
- JSON should include same info as text output, just structured
- Error handling: should errors also be JSON when --json is set?
  Decision: Yes, { 'error': '...', 'code': N } envelope

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Audit document complete
- All data-producing commands have --json
- Consistent envelope schema
- Documentation updated

RATIONALE: Scripting/automation needs reliable machine-readable output. Inconsistency is a paper cut. Low effort, high polish.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T20:19:55.239400136-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T21:39:10.605432524-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T21:39:10.605432524-05:00","close_reason":"Added --json to ls, status, activate, backup commands; cba06db, 60d5ff8","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-04mb","depends_on_id":"caam-z7j7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T20:28:13.609563022-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-04mb","depends_on_id":"caam-9a3c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T20:28:18.719978482-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"caam-05i","title":"E2E: Profile backup and restore workflow","description":"End-to-end tests for complete backup/restore workflow:

## Test Scenarios
1. Full backup workflow:
   - Create temp HOME directory
   - Place mock auth files for a provider
   - Run backup command
   - Verify profile directory structure created
   - Verify auth files copied correctly

2. Full restore workflow:
   - Start with empty auth state
   - Run restore command
   - Verify auth files restored to correct locations
   - Verify provider recognizes restored auth

3. Backup overwrite protection:
   - Attempt to backup existing profile
   - Verify confirmation required
   - Test --force flag behavior

4. Cross-provider workflows:
   - Backup Claude profile
   - Backup Codex profile  
   - Switch between them
   - Verify isolation

## Requirements
- Test with real file system operations
- No mocking of authfile or profile packages
- Detailed step-by-step logging
- Measure and log execution times","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:52:46.775884454-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T02:10:57.438022631-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T02:10:57.438022631-05:00","close_reason":"Completed 9 E2E tests for backup/restore workflow: full backup, full restore, overwrite protection, cross-provider isolation, round-trip, multiple profiles, error handling.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-05i","depends_on_id":"caam-7a2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:54:58.925610007-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-05i","depends_on_id":"caam-aek","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:55:31.189135314-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"caam-08w","title":"Profile aliases and fuzzy matching","description":"## Problem
Profile names like "gptpro-account-work-2" are hard to remember and type.

## Solution
Allow short aliases and fuzzy matching.

## Features

### 1. Profile Aliases
```bash
caam alias claude work-account-1 work
caam alias claude personal-gmail-max home

# Now use short names:
caam activate claude work
caam next claude  # Shows: work, home
```

### 2. Fuzzy Matching
```bash
caam activate claude w     # Matches "work" or "work-account-1"
caam activate claude per   # Matches "personal-gmail-max"
```

### 3. Default/Favorite Profiles
```bash
caam favorite claude work home  # Mark as favorites
caam next claude               # Rotates through favorites first
```

## Storage
```json
{
  "aliases": {
    "claude/work-account-1": ["work", "w"],
    "claude/personal": ["home", "h", "personal"]
  },
  "favorites": {
    "claude": ["work-account-1", "personal"]
  }
}
```

## CLI
```bash
caam alias <tool> <profile> <alias>   # Add alias
caam alias --list                      # List all aliases
caam alias --remove <alias>            # Remove alias
caam favorite <tool> <profiles...>     # Set favorites
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T19:00:37.796787887-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T21:22:37.235502557-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T21:22:37.235502557-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-08w","depends_on_id":"caam-m9g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T19:03:28.342819681-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

}

 {"id":"caam-0ds","title":"DB stats: keep last_error monotonic","description":"profile_stats.last_error is updated with the newest inserted error event, but can move backwards if events are written out-of-order (e.g., backfills). Use MAX(last_error, excluded.last_error) like last_activated.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T02:12:30.331656689-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T02:17:48.324292702-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T02:17:48.324292702-05:00","close_reason":"profile_stats.last_error now updates with MAX()+COALESCE to avoid NULL pitfalls and timestamp regressions; added regression test.","labels":["analytics","db"],"comments":[{"id":70,"issue_id":"caam-0ds","author":"ubuntu","text":"Claimed: align profile_stats.last_error update with last_activated by using MAX() so stats stay monotonic under out-of-order inserts.","created_at":"2025-12-18T07:13:01Z"},{"id":72,"issue_id":"caam-0ds","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fixed profile_stats.last_error monotonicity: use MAX(COALESCE(...)) to handle NULL and prevent regressions under out-of-order inserts; added regression test. go test ./... and go test -race ./... pass.","created_at":"2025-12-18T07:17:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-0dy","title":"Unit Test Coverage","description":"## Overview
Add unit tests for all internal packages without using mocks or fakes.

## Packages Needing Tests (ordered by dependency)
1. internal/version - Simple, no dependencies
2. internal/config - Configuration loading
3. internal/authfile - Auth file operations (core!)
4. internal/provider - Provider interface and registry
5. internal/provider/claude - Claude-specific provider
6. internal/provider/codex - Codex-specific provider  
7. internal/provider/gemini - Gemini-specific provider
8. internal/profile - Profile management (vault + isolation)
9. internal/passthrough - Symlink passthrough system
10. internal/browser - Browser profile management
11. internal/exec - Command execution runner
12. cmd/caam/cmd - CLI command tests

## Testing Philosophy
- Use t.TempDir() for isolated file operations
- Test real file system behavior
- No mocks - use actual implementations
- Table-driven tests for edge cases
- Clear test names describing behavior","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:47:58.975749117-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:59:13.278063161-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:59:13.278063161-05:00","close_reason":"Unit test coverage epic: authfile and profile tests complete (82%+). Pattern established for remaining packages.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-0dy","depends_on_id":"caam-9c4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:53:38.591935987-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"caam-0eq","title":"Health status display in CLI","description":"# Health Status Display in CLI

## Purpose
Show profile health status indicators in CLI commands like `caam list` and `caam status`.

## User Experience Goals
Users should see at a glance:
- Which profiles are healthy
- Which profiles have issues
- What the issues are (expiring, errors, etc.)

## Display Formats

### `caam list` Output
```
$ caam list
Claude Profiles
  ● work@company.com      🟢 Pro    59m left   [ACTIVE]
    personal@gmail.com    🟡 Free   12m left   
    test@example.com      🔴 Expired            

Codex Profiles
  ● main                  🟢        23h left   [ACTIVE]
    backup                ⚪ Unknown            

Gemini Profiles
    default               🟢        Valid
```

### `caam status` Output
```
$ caam status
Active Profiles
───────────────────────────────────────────────────
claude    work@company.com    🟢 Healthy    59m left
codex     main                🟢 Healthy    23h left
gemini    (none)              

Warnings
───────────────────────────────────────────────────
claude/personal@gmail.com: Token expires in 12 minutes
claude/test@example.com: Token expired

Recommendations
───────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Run "caam refresh claude personal@gmail.com" to refresh expiring token
• Run "caam login claude test@example.com" to re-authenticate
```

### Status Column Format
```
🟢 59m left     - Healthy with time remaining
🟡 12m left     - Warning, expiring soon
🔴 Expired      - Critical, needs attention
🔴 3 errors     - Critical, multiple recent errors
⚪ Unknown      - Cannot determine status
```

## Technical Requirements

### Integration Points
```go
// In list command
profiles := store.List(provider)
for _, p := range profiles {
    health := healthStore.GetProfile(provider, p.Name)
    status, _ := health.CalculateHealth()
    fmt.Printf("  %s %s %s\n", 
        activeIndicator(p),
        p.Name,
        formatHealthStatus(status, health))
}
```

### Helper Functions
```go
func formatHealthStatus(status HealthStatus, h *ProfileHealth) string
func formatTimeRemaining(expiry time.Time) string
func formatStatusWithReason(status HealthStatus, h *ProfileHealth) string
```

## Color Support
Use lipgloss styles consistent with TUI:
- 🟢 Green for healthy
- 🟡 Yellow for warning
- 🔴 Red for critical
- Gray for unknown

Support `--no-color` flag for piping.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `caam list` shows health status for each profile
- [ ] `caam status` shows active profiles with health
- [ ] `caam status` shows warnings and recommendations
- [ ] Time remaining formatted naturally (59m, 23h, 3d)
- [ ] Colors disabled with --no-color or non-TTY
- [ ] Unit tests for formatting functions

## Files to Create/Modify
- `cmd/caam/cmd/list.go` (modify)
- `cmd/caam/cmd/status.go` (modify)
- `internal/health/format.go` (new)
- `internal/health/format_test.go` (new)

## Dependencies
- Health status calculation (caam-3ef)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:18:49.689326323-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:26:54.046325238-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:26:54.046325238-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented health status display in CLI. Updated status and ls commands to show detailed health info using HealthStore with fallback to parsing.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-0eq","depends_on_id":"caam-3ef","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:27:37.521450734-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
